steps.title: Welcome to Day 12
steps.body: AI is incredible at research — if you use it right. Today you learn the research workflow: find information, cross-check it, and organize it into something useful.

steps.title: Research prompt
steps.prompt: I am researching [topic]. I need a clear overview covering: what it is, why it matters, who the key players are, and what is changing right now. For each point, include specific examples and tell me how confident you are in each claim.

steps.title: The research workflow
steps.body: Step 1: Ask AI for a broad overview. Step 2: Ask follow-up questions on specific areas. Step 3: Ask AI for sources and counterpoints. Step 4: Cross-check critical facts yourself. Step 5: Ask AI to organize everything into a summary.

steps.title: Why cross-check?
steps.question: Why should you cross-check AI research with other sources?
options: AI can be confidently wrong or make things up
AI does not have access to the internet
feedback: AI is not always wrong, but it is not always right either.
Exactly. AI hallucinates — it sounds convincing even when it invents facts.
Cross-checking is essential for any important research.
Modern AI tools can search the internet, but the hallucination risk remains.

steps.title: A real research session
steps.body: You need to research the best accounting software for your small business. Without AI: hours of Googling, comparing features, reading reviews. With AI: ask for a comparison, then cross-check two claims that matter most to you.

steps.title: Build a research prompt

fields.label: What do you want to research?
fields.placeholder: best project management tools for a small team

fields.label: What specific questions do you need answered?
What format would you like the answer in?

steps.template: Research {{topic}}. Focus on {{focus}}. Present the answer as {{format}}. For each claim, indicate your confidence level (high/medium/low). After the main answer, suggest 2-3 things I should verify myself before making a decision.

steps.title: Save your research
steps.body: Paste the research results AI gave you. You will cross-check them next.

steps.title: How to cross-check
steps.body: Pick the 2-3 most critical claims from AI's output. Ask AI: 'Where did you get that information?' Ask a separate AI tool: 'Can you verify that [claim] is true?' Then do one quick Google search on the most important point. That is enough.

steps.title: Spot the cross-check worthy claims
steps.body: Which of these AI claims should you definitely verify? '95% of businesses use this software.' 'The CEO's name is John Smith.' 'This tool costs $29/month.' 'The stock market opens at 9:30 AM.' 'All competitors charge less.'

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: If AI sounds confident and provides specific numbers, those numbers are always reliable.
feedback: Confidence and accuracy are unrelated in AI. Always verify specific numbers.
Right. AI sounds confident even when it is wrong.

steps.title: Organizing research
steps.body: After you have the information, ask AI: 'Organize this into a short briefing document. Include: key findings, what I should verify, what questions are still open, and my recommended next steps.' This turns research into action.

questions.label: What is the next topic you want to research with AI?
How will you verify AI's most important claims?

steps.title: Good for AI research?
steps.question: Compare the top 3 CRM tools for my real estate business.
feedback: Yes. Comparative research is one of AI's best use cases.
Exactly. Just verify the pricing and specific feature claims.

steps.title: Good for AI research?
steps.question: Tell me exactly what my medical test results mean and whether I need treatment.
feedback: AI can give general information, but medical interpretation needs a doctor.
Right. Use AI for background research, not medical diagnosis.

questions.question: What is hallucination in AI?
options: AI making up confident-sounding false information

questions.question: You should cross-check the most critical claims AI makes.
The process of verifying AI's claims against other sources is called ______.

steps.title: Day 12 complete — Research Pro
steps.body: You now know how to research with AI, verify critical claims, and organize findings. Tomorrow: using AI to prepare for meetings, calls, and interviews.
